Content_Sketch: Stethoscope on chest. Heartbeat steady. You write 'unremarkable' on the form.
Illustration_Note: Close-up: stickman doctor placing stethoscope on patient chest, clipboard in other hand, clinical exam room

Content_Sketch: Same equipment. Different purpose. Same hands. Only the room changes.
Illustration_Note: Split frame: identical medical equipment in two rooms - one bright clinic, one dim back office

Content_Sketch: You do not remember which room you are in. It does not matter anymore.
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman standing alone between two doors, lab coat identical in both frames

Content_Sketch: You are twenty-nine. Medical school debt compounds faster than your salary.
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman at desk with stack of bills, hospital corridor in background

Content_Sketch: The broker calls you Doctor. Not your name. He has a simple request. Blood test. Compatibility check. No one will know.
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman holding phone, shadow figure on other end, evening light

Content_Sketch: The exam room is at the end of an alley. The equipment is old but functional. The light is yellow.
Illustration_Note: Dim back-room clinic setup: exam chair, basic equipment, single overhead light

Content_Sketch: The first donor sits across from you. Eyes down. You place the stethoscope. Your hand trembles. You check twice.
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman with trembling hand placing stethoscope on thin donor stickman (dot eyes + fearful mouth), exam room

Content_Sketch: You sign the form. You drive home. You wash your hands three times before you sleep.
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman at sink washing hands, lab coat hanging on door, night outside window

Content_Sketch: Morning shift. A patient sits on your table. You listen. You write 'within range.' She says thank you.
Illustration_Note: Bright legitimate clinic: doctor stickman examining patient (faceless), clipboard, professional setting

Content_Sketch: Evening. A donor sits on a different table. You listen. You write 'within range.' Same tone. He says nothing.
Illustration_Note: Split frame: morning clinic (patient says thank you) vs evening back office (donor silent), identical doctor posture

Content_Sketch: You stop checking twice. Then you stop checking once. The stethoscope still works the same way.
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman placing stethoscope casually, no hesitation, quicker motion than EARLY phase

Content_Sketch: You slide the sample between two real patients on the lab tray. Your handwriting on the false name is steady now.
Illustration_Note: Close-up: lab tray with three sample tubes, middle one with different label, doctor hand placing it

Content_Sketch: A donor asks: is this safe? You answer the way you practiced. Same sentence. Same pause before the word 'yes.'
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman facing donor (faceless), mouth open speaking, donor seated on exam chair

Content_Sketch: The checkbox on the form. You hesitate before marking it. Not because you do not know. Because you do.
Illustration_Note: Extreme close-up: pen hovering over checkbox on medical form, doctor hand gripping pen

Content_Sketch: You mark it. Next time you do not hesitate. The pen moves the same distance either way.
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman signing form without looking up, pen moving quickly, stack of completed forms beside

Content_Sketch: A new sample arrives. The volume is smaller than usual. You do not ask why.
Illustration_Note: Close-up: small blood sample vial in doctor's hand, noticeably smaller than usual

Content_Sketch: The broker calls. Results? You look at the numbers on the paper. You say no. First time.
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman on phone, holding lab results, mouth saying no, broker shadow on other end

Content_Sketch: One week later. The broker calls again. Another doctor cleared them. The system does not need your permission. It needs a permission.
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman alone, phone down, staring at wall, realization

Content_Sketch: Next call. You do not check the volume. You read the blood type. You read the kidney function. You sign.
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman signing form mechanically, not looking at sample, pen moving without pause

Content_Sketch: You stop reading the name on the form. Blood type. Kidney function. Compatibility. That is all you need.
Illustration_Note: Close-up: form with name field blurred/ignored, doctor eyes on data fields only

Content_Sketch: Morning: stethoscope, notes, signature. Evening: stethoscope, notes, signature. Same hands. Same pen. Same form.
Illustration_Note: Side-by-side: morning clinic and evening back office - doctor performing identical sequence in both

Content_Sketch: You did not become a monster. You just stopped noticing the difference.
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman walking through corridor, lab coat, blank expression, indistinguishable setting

Content_Sketch: Someone asks what you do. You say: doctor. Nothing more. Nothing less.
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman in casual setting, talking to someone (faceless), calm neutral posture

Content_Sketch: Stethoscope on chest. Heartbeat steady. You write 'unremarkable.' No abnormalities detected.
Illustration_Note: Mirror of HOOK: identical composition - stethoscope on chest, clipboard, pen writing 'unremarkable'

Content_Sketch: You walk into the room. You do not remember which one.
Illustration_Note: Doctor stickman stepping through doorway into ambiguous room - could be clinic or back office, indistinguishable
